ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Qian Ceng You Gao (Chinese steamed layered cake). FOODON_03315091 (steamed cake food product) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Steamed cake) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for Chinese-style layered steamed cake (Qian Ceng You Gao) in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Qian Ceng You Gao or Chinese steamed layered cake. SNOMED CT coverage of regional Asian dessert pastries is limited.

serving.description: 1 portion (approx. 100 g slice from 500 g pack)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is approximately 100 g, representing one-fifth of the 500 g frozen pack, equivalent to a generous slice of the layered steamed cake.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cake, white, prepared from recipe without frosting (SR Legacy ID 18101); adapted for Chinese steamed layered cake composition
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Qian Ceng You Gao (Chinese steamed layered cake) exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry for plain white cake (ID 18101) with adjustments reflecting the steamed preparation method (lower fat than baked cake, higher moisture), typical lard/shortening content in traditional Qian Ceng You Gao, and published compositional analyses of similar Chinese steamed pastries. Energy and macros are cross-referenced against FAO/INFOODS SEA data for steamed wheat-flour-based desserts. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Sponge cake (plain, steamed/baked wheat flour cake) GI reference value 67 (glucose=100).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Qian Ceng You Gao (Chinese steamed layered cake). GI of 67 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain sponge cake / white wheat-flour cake (GI 67, glucose reference), which is the closest analogous food in terms of primary ingredients (refined wheat flour, sugar, eggs, fat) and preparation method (steaming/baking). Glycemic load per 100 g serving = (67 × 50.2) / 100 = 33.6. This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~180 mg/100 g) and refined carbohydrate load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one small slice (≤80 g) and account for the 144 mg sodium contribution within a daily target of under 2000 mg; the refined carbohydrate content may also indirectly elevate blood pressure through insulin-mediated sodium retention.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (67) and high glycemic load (~33.6 per 100 g serving) driven by refined wheat flour and added sugar (~22 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as an occasional indulgence only; a 100 g serving delivers approximately 50 g of rapidly digestible carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~34, which will produce a significant postprandial glucose excursion and should be paired with protein or fibre to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~2.8 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g), cholesterol (~45 mg/100 g), and high refined sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this cake due to its saturated fat, trace trans fat, and high added sugar content, all of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles; a single 100 g serving contributes approximately 14% of the recommended daily saturated fat limit (20 g) and should be consumed infrequently as part of a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Qian Ceng You Gao (千层油糕), literally 'thousand-layer oil cake', is a traditional Chinese steamed layered dessert originating from the Jiangsu and Shanghai regions of China. It is a classic dim sum dessert widely served in Cantonese and Shanghainese restaurants across Singapore and Southeast Asia. The cake is made from refined wheat flour, sugar, lard or vegetable shortening, eggs, and baking powder, steamed in a large tray and then cut into diamond or rectangular portions. Its defining characteristic is its soft, moist, slightly chewy texture with visible alternating pale and yellow layers, the yellow colour traditionally derived from egg yolk or food colouring. The flavour is mildly sweet with a subtle richness from the fat content. Din Tai Fung's version, sold as a 500 g frozen product in Singapore, represents a triple-sized portion of this classic dessert, intended to be reheated by steaming at home. Nutritionally, each 100 g serving provides approximately 285 kcal, 50 g of carbohydrate (of which ~22 g is sugar), 7.5 g of fat, and 4.8 g of protein. The glycemic index is estimated at 67 (medium-high), making it a high glycemic load food unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing blood glucose. The sodium content (~180 mg/100 g) is moderate. This food is vegetarian but not vegan (contains eggs and may contain lard). It is not gluten-free. Clinically, it is best treated as an occasional treat for individuals with metabolic conditions.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for Qian Ceng You Gao (Chinese steamed layered cake) was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy plain white cake (ID 18101) with adjustments for steamed preparation and traditional fat composition. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using plain sponge/white cake as the closest reference food. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated/adapted from USDA SR Legacy ID 18101 (plain white cake) and FAO/INFOODS SEA steamed wheat-flour dessert references; direct laboratory analysis of Qian Ceng You Gao is recommended for high-confidence classification. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy source for this cake category. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy source for this cake category. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy source for this cake category. 5. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate based on plain sponge cake from the University of Sydney GI Database; direct GI measurement for Qian Ceng You Gao is not available in peer-reviewed literature. 6. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero as steamed wheat-flour-and-sugar cake contains no vitamin C by composition. 7. Halal status is uncertain as traditional recipes use lard (pork fat); Din Tai Fung Singapore's halal certification status should be verified independently. 8. The 500 g frozen product format is a retail/restaurant take-home product; nutrient values apply to the steamed cake component per 100 g as consumed after reheating.
